name: City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional
name_original: City tour Alcántara – circular por Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcántara, Extremadura, Spain, is characterized by routes along the Tajo riverbed and the large Alcántara reservoir. The landscape features extensive cork-oak and holm-oak forests, particularly on the foothills of the Sierra de San Pedro and Sierra de Gata. Many routes traverse the Tajo Internacional Natural Park, offering diverse ecosystems and undulating terrain with varying elevations. The region provides a mix of paved and unpaved segments suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alcántara The most popular touring cycling route is Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop, a 16.9 miles (27.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate cycling experience through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional, a difficult 18.5 miles (29.8 km) path. It features significant elevation gain around the reservoir and through the natural park. Local touring cyclists also love the Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcántara is defined by routes along the Tajo River, through cork-oak forests, and past historical landmarks like the Roman Bridge of Alcántara. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 45 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alcántara, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Alcántara offers 16 touring cycling routes. You'll find a good variety, with 2 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 7 more challenging difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain and natural features can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alcántara?
A: The routes in Alcántara are characterized by undulating terrain, with varying elevations through extensive cork-oak and holm-oak forests. Many paths follow the Tajo riverbed and the large Alcántara reservoir, often traversing the beautiful Tajo Internacional Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved segments.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alcántara?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.9-mile (27.2 km) option. Another popular choice is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114013055/city-tour-alcantara-circular-by-embalse-de-jose-maria-de-oriol-alcantara-ii-parque-natural-del-tagus-internacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour Alcántara – circular by Embalse de José María de Oriol - Alcántara II – Parque Natural del Tagus Internacional&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 18.5 miles (29.8 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. A prominent landmark is the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an engineering marvel from the second century. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roco-Campofrío Palace&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Almocóvar Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions to look out for?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the José María de Oriol - Alcántara II Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often wind through the Tajo Internacional Natural Park, offering diverse ecosystems and opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcántara?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic routes along the Tajo River and reservoir, and the chance to explore historical landmarks like the Roman Bridge.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Alcántara offers 2 easy touring cycling routes. While specific names for these easy routes aren't highlighted, the overall network provides options for various ability levels, ensuring there are less demanding paths for those new to touring cycling or seeking a relaxed ride.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through rural towns or villages?
A: Many routes connect to or pass through traditional towns and rural landscapes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114110581/brozas-and-navas-del-madrono-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through rural areas, offering a glimpse into the local countryside and potentially passing through the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alcántara?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the cork-oak and holm-oak forests.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options available?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40004864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floripes Tower – Río Tajo Train Station loop from Garrovillas de Alconétar&lt;/a&gt; cover a substantial distance of 81.4 miles (131 km) with significant elevation gain, connecting various points of interest.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a challenging experience for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenges, with 7 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real de Gata loop from Piedras Albas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.2-mile (29.3 km) route with considerable elevation, providing a demanding ride through the landscape.
tips: Built over the Tagus River near present-day Alcántara, in the province of Cáceres, the Alcántara Bridge is one of the most beautiful and important examples of Roman civil engineering. Despite being shorter than that of Mérida and with fewer arches, it is much higher and above all more impressive. The complex is made up of three distinct parts: the bridge, the central triumphal arch and a small rectangular temple measuring 5.60 x 4.10 meters. This building consists of a staircase, two Tuscan columns, an entablature and a classical pediment. A restored plaque indicates the name of its builder and reminds us that the bridge will last “as long as the world lasts”:

If there is something that we always like, it is viewpoints and in this town we have several to have different views of the bridge. For example, the Mirador del Puente which is a little beyond the Convent of the Commanders of Sancti Spiritus and from which we have almost top-down views of the bridge.
Incredible views from the north side of the reservoir.

A place that could well be paradise in summer, it is one of the most beautiful bathing areas in Extremadura.
When the Alcántara reservoir was built, too many stones were needed to build the dam. Many came out of this quarry

Built in the 16th century, this historic building is in the Renaissance style with a Gothic cloister and three naves with Plateresque ornamentation inside. It was restored by the Iberdrola Spain Foundation and currently hosts frequent cultural activities such as English courses for students and teachers in the community or the Classical Theater Festival.

To learn in detail about this monument and its more than five centuries of history, they organize free guided tours thanks to the Iberdrola foundation.
There is a gravel path along the winding banks of the José María de Oriol - Alcántara II reservoir. Water edge levels may differ in the dry season.
